title: quercus stellata var. drummondii
intro: Quercus stellata var. drummondii, also known as the prairie bur oak, is a deciduous tree species native to North America, characterized by its distinctive star-shaped leaves and broad, rounded crown. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The leaves and bark of Quercus stellata var. drummondii have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their rich content of tannins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
